alternative_explanations: Strict-past percentile nonuniformity can arise from time-series autocorrelation, regime drift, ties, and finite rolling windows; this affects nominal-q coverage but does not invalidate the fixed threshold definition.
C layer near-redundancy reflects two related reference-price spread constructions; it remains below the preregistered redundancy boundary and retains nonzero A-only and B-only samples.
V layer W-dependent identity and T q10 joint fragmentation are material structure warnings for downstream interpretation, not evidence for changing W, q, or indicator membership inside R1-T05.
anomaly_review: All mandatory anomaly checks were read from the committed scan. The revised run records no blocking anomalies. Nonblocking material warnings are explicit in the anomaly scan and result analysis, including C_layer_near_redundancy, V_layer_W_dependent_identity, T_layer_q10_joint_high_fragmentation, T2_AbsTrendT20_extreme_right_tail, nonuniform strict-past percentile buckets, and nominal-q coverage divergence.
baseline_challenger_review: Reviewed the fixed W={120,250,500} and q={0.10,0.20,0.30} grid without selecting a winner. At W250/q20, C, P, T, and V all retain nonzero both-hit, A-only, and B-only cells, and all 12 layer-window classifications remain complementary_structure.

independent_recomputations.metric: T W250/q10 joint single-day fragment ratio

parameter_response_review: Reviewed W availability monotonicity, q hit nesting, percentile bucket conservation, hit denominator integrity, joint segment interruption across pair-ineligible gaps, validity reason denominator semantics, score=1-percentile reconciliation, and R0-T06 active-state reconciliation. The revised implementation and committed artifacts address the previous blocking findings.
